leadText: If you were looking for the skill in Pokémon Shuffle, see Skill (Shuffle) → Rock Break. Rock Smash (Japanese: いわくだき Rock Smash) is a damage-dealing Fighting-type move introduced in Generation II. It is: TM08 in Generation II. HM06 in Generations III and IV. TM94 in Generation V and Pokémon X and Y. HM06 in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire. TM98 in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM004 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generations II and III 1.1.2 Generation IV onwards 1.1.3 Generation VIII 1.1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.2 Generation IX 1.2.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A 1.3 Outside of battle 1.3.1 Generations II and III 1.3.2 Generation IV 1.3.2.1 Diamond, Pearl and Platinum 1.3.2.2 HeartGold and SoulSilver 1.3.3 Generation V 1.3.4 Generation VI 1.3.5 Generation VII 1.3.6 Generation VIII 1.3.7 Generation IX 1.3.8 Puzzles involving Rock Smash Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations II and III [ edit | edit source ] The move inflicts damage and has a 50% chance of lowering the target's Defense stat by one stage. Rock Smash has a base power of 20. Generation IV onwards [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash now has a base power of 40. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Physical PP 20 Agile Strong Power 40 30 50 Accuracy 100% 100% 100% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect Chance 50% 50% 100% Turns 3 2 4 ? ? May lower the target's defensive stats Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Physical Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 40 48 Cooldown 6s Move wind-up time 10 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect Chance 50% Duration 8s 11s — May lower the target's Defense. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] In certain games, Rock Smash requires a specific badge to use outside of battle; the Marsh Badge FR LG , Zephyr Badge HG SS , Dynamo Badge R S E OR AS , or Coal Badge D P Pt BD SP . Generations II and III [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash can be used to break breakable rocks to clear a path. Occasionally, breaking a rock will cause a wild Pokémon to be encountered. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Diamond, Pearl and Platinum [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash can be used to break breakable rocks, but no wild Pokémon will appear. HeartGold and SoulSilver [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash can be used to break breakable rocks, and wild Pokémon or items can be found. Items that can be found include Heart Scales , shards , and Fossils . Generation V [ edit | edit source ] Boulders in Clay Tunnel Rock Smash is no longer a field move . In Black 2 and White 2 , if a Pokémon in the player's party knows the move Rock Smash when they talk to one of the Workers in Clay Tunnel , they can help clear a section of boulders. Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash can be used to break breakable rocks and certain cracked walls. Wild Pokémon and items can be found when breaking rocks (but not cracked walls). Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash can no longer be used outside of battle. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash is a field move in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl , usable via the Pokétch 's Hidden Moves app, which summons a wild Bidoof to use the move. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Rock Smash can no longer be used outside of battle. Puzzles involving Rock Smash [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Emerald , using Rock Smash in the Desert Ruins grants access to a chamber where the player finds Regirock .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad2 A Fighting -type attack. In the field, it can be used to shatter rocks and open new paths. G S C An attack that may lower Defense . R S E A rock-crushing attack that may lower Defense . (move) A rock-crushingly tough attack that may lower Defense . * FR LG An attack that may also cut Defense . It can also smash cracked boulders. Colo XD A rock-crushing attack that may lower Defense . D P Pt The user slugs the foe with a shattering punch. It can also smash cracked boulders. PBR The user slugs the foe with a shattering punch. HG SS The user hits with a punch that may lower the target's Defense. It can also smash cracked boulders. (move) The user slugs the foe with a shattering punch. It can also smash cracked boulders. * B W B2 W2 The user attacks with a punch that can shatter a rock. It may also lower the target's Defense stat. X Y OR AS The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower the target's Defense stat. This move can also shatter rocks in the field. S M US UM P E Sw Sh S V The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower the target's Defense stat. BD SP The user attacks with a punch that may lower the target’s Defense stat. It’s also one of the Pokétch’s hidden moves. LA The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower the target’s defensive stats. ZA The user attacks with a punch. This may also lower targets' Defense stats.
